title: 15 Depositing Cheques in Corporate Capture
body_text:
15	Depositing Cheques in Corporate Capture
15.1 	Overview of Depositing Cheques in Corporate Capture
Corporate Capture allows your business customers to securely scan Canadian and US dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders and transmit them to Central 1 for processing.
Corporate Capture supports real-time balance updating via Central 1芒聙聶s Real-Time Engine. Settlement is via an automated end-of-day deposit file or a manual settlement process. Central 1 provides a Corporate Capture Deposit File that芒聙聶s used for reconciliation and, if applicable, for settlement. See Section 15.11, Corporate Capture Deposit File.
Your financial institution can implement Corporate Capture without banking host changes, but they are required for real-time balance updating and automated end-of-day settlement with the business customer. Without banking host changes, all end-of-day settlement is a manual process. Corporate Capture deposits must be transmitted to Central 1 by 7:00 pm local time or they are processed the following business day. Business customers must scan and transmit deposits throughout the day. Ensure that they don芒聙聶t transmit all deposits to Central 1 at the end of day.
See the Corporate Capture user guides for procedures on using the Corporate Capture application. Corporate Capture user guides are user-specific (e.g., Capture User, Analyst, Supervisor) and can be provided to participating business customers.
Training videos are available on the Client Centre.
Related Information
Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Analyst User Guide,
Form 5930
Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Capture and Supervisor User Guide,
Form 5931
Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Capture User Guide,
Form 5932
Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Supervisor User Guide,
Form 5934
Training Videos: Corporate Capture
15.2	Enrolling in Corporate Capture
Your financial institution must enter into an agreement with Central 1 to use Corporate Capture. Contact Central 1, Client Support Services, to enroll your financial institution in Corporate Capture. Central 1 will work with your financial institution to implement the application. See Section 3.7, Agreements for Corporate Capture Deposit Processing, for more information.
15.3	Corporate Capture Workflows and User Types
Your financial institution can elect to have business customers scan and transmit Corporate Capture deposits directly to Central 1 for processing, or your staff can review deposits and transmit them to Central 1 on your business customers芒聙聶 behalf.
There are three Corporate Capture user types: Corporate Deposit Capture (Capture User), Corporate Deposit Supervisor (Supervisor User), and Corporate Deposit Analyst (Analyst User). Based on how you assign user types, the user芒聙聶s tasks, experiences, and responsibilities vary. The Corporate Deposit Capture user type is always assigned to business customer users. You decide which workflow or combination of workflows to use for your business customers. For example, you can have some business customers that perform all the deposit processing tasks while others create deposits and then submit them to your financial institution for additional processing.
Important
: 	If your financial institution chooses to review and transmit Corporate Capture deposits on your business customers芒聙聶 behalf, ensure that staff who process Corporate Capture deposits aren芒聙聶t Branch Capture Users. The Corporate Capture application and Branch Capture application share common attributes but have different workflows.
There isn芒聙聶t a Corporate Capture Administrator user type. Business customers that you enroll in Corporate Capture essentially become an extension of your financial institution. As a result, your existing Branch Capture Administrator Users can generate reports for all business customer locations.
This section describes Corporate Capture user types and their associated workflows, responsibilities, benefits, and considerations. Use these descriptions to evaluate your financial institution芒聙聶s needs and to determine how best to assign Corporate Capture user types. See the User Management Part for instructions on setting up Corporate Capture Users.
Option 1: Separate User Types 芒聙聯 Financial Institution Monitors Deposits
This workflow allows for the separation of the business user芒聙聶s deposit tasks and minimizes your participation in the deposit process. Authorized Analyst Users at your financial institution can view the deposits of assigned business locations. See Figure 1 for a diagram of this workflow.
Benefits
芒聙聯
A Supervisor User must review Capture User芒聙聶s deposits. This helps ensure deposits are error-free and balanced before submitting them to Central 1 for processing. If an error is detected, the Supervisor can disapprove the deposit for a Capture User to correct.
Note:	Real-time deposits to the business customer芒聙聶s account (if implemented) won芒聙聶t occur until the Supervisor User reviews and transmits the deposit.
Considerations
芒聙聯 As your financial institution isn芒聙聶t involved in the deposit process, responsibility for ensuring deposits are error-free and balanced is transferred to business customers. Ensure that business users understand their roles and responsibilities. Deposits containing errors are subject to deposit adjustments. See Section 17.3, Deposit Adjustments.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
User Types Assigned
Assigned to
User Responsibilities
Corporate Deposit Capture (Capture User)
Business Customer
Creates deposits and scans deposit items
Ensures deposits are error-free and balanced
Completes deposits for review by a Supervisor User at the business
The Capture User selects
Complete
when the deposit is error-free and balanced.
The deposit can be edited and corrected if disapproved.
The deposit isn芒聙聶t submitted for processing until a Supervisor User at the business reviews and transmits it.
Corporate Deposit Supervisor (Supervisor User)
Business Customer
Reviews deposits for errors
Disapproves deposits with errors for Capture Users to correct
Reassigns deposits that haven芒聙聶t been completed (if required)
Transmits deposits to Central 1 for processing
Generates deposit reports for the business
Manages Aged Open deposits for the business
Corporate Deposit Analyst (Analyst User)
Financial Institution
Note: This user type can also be assigned to an individual at the business who requires view-only access to deposits. Analyst Users can also create deposit reports for the business.
Views deposits for assigned business customers
Generates deposit reports for assigned business customers
Figure 1: Separate Capture and Supervisor Users (Business Customer) and Analyst (Financial Institution)
Option 2: Separate User Types 芒聙聯 Financial Institution Reviews and Transmits Deposits
This workflow allows for the separation of business users芒聙聶 deposit and reporting tasks but requires your financial institution to participate in the deposit process. Authorized users at your financial institution must review and transmit deposits created by assigned business customers. See Figure 2. for a diagram of this workflow.
Benefits
芒聙聯 You can monitor your business customer芒聙聶s deposits to ensure they are error-free and balanced before they are submitted to Central 1 for processing. If you detect an error, the deposit can be disapproved for a Capture User at the business to correct.
Considerations
芒聙聯 Real-time deposits (if implemented) won芒聙聶t occur for the business customer until the Supervisor User at your financial institution that芒聙聶s assigned to the business customer reviews and transmits the deposit. Your financial institution芒聙聶s Supervisor Users must monitor their Corporate Capture deposit list queues for business customer deposits regularly throughout the day.

Assigned to
User Responsibilities
Corporate Deposit Capture (Capture User)
Business Customer
Creates deposits and scans deposit items
Ensures deposits are error-free and balanced
Completes deposits for review by a Supervisor User

Complete
when the deposit is error-free and balanced.
The deposit can be edited.
The deposit isn芒聙聶t transmitted for processing until a financial institution Supervisor User reviews and transmits it.
Corporate Deposit Analyst (Analyst User)
Business Customer
Views deposits made by all Corporate Capture users at the business
Generates deposit reports for the business
Corporate Deposit Supervisor (Supervisor User)
Financial Institution
Reviews deposits for errors on behalf of assigned business customers
Disapproves deposits with errors for Capture Users at the business to correct
Reassigns deposits that haven芒聙聶t been completed (if required)
Transmits deposits on behalf of assigned business customers
Generates deposit reports for assigned business customers
Manages Aged Open deposits for assigned business customers
Figure 2: Separate Capture and Analyst Users (Business Customer) and Supervisor (Financial Institution)
Option 3:	Combined User Types 芒聙聯 Financial Institution Monitors Deposits
This workflow allows for business user芒聙聶s deposit tasks to be combined into one role and minimizes your financial institution芒聙聶s participation in the deposit process. See Figure 3 for a diagram of this workflow.
Benefits
芒聙聯 	Individual business users perform all deposit processing tasks. Real-time deposit (if implemented) occurs for the business customer after the business user completes the deposit. Capture and Supervisor Users can review, disapprove, and transmit deposit for Capture Users at the business customer芒聙聶s location (if applicable).
Considerations
芒聙聯 As your financial institution isn芒聙聶t involved in the deposit process, responsibility for ensuring deposits are error-free and balanced is transferred to business customers. Ensure users understand their roles and responsibilities. Deposits containing errors are subject to deposit adjustments. See Section 17.3 Deposit Adjustments.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.

when the deposit is error-free and balanced.

Financial Institution

Views deposits at assigned business customer locations

Option 4: Combined User Types 芒聙聯 Financial Institution Reviews and Transmits Deposits
This workflow allows for the business user芒聙聶s deposit and reporting tasks to be combined into one role but requires your financial institution to participate in the deposit process. Authorized users at your financial institution must review and transmit deposits created by assigned business locations. See Figure 4 for a diagram of this workflow.
Benefits
芒聙聯 Financial institutions can monitor business customer芒聙聶s deposits to ensure they are error-free and balanced before they are submitted to Central 1 for processing. If an error is detected, the deposit can be disapproved for a Capture User to correct.
Considerations
芒聙聯 Real-time deposits (if implemented) won芒聙聶t occur for the business customer until the Corporate Deposit Supervisor at your financial institution reviews and transmits the deposit. Financial institution芒聙聶s Supervisor Users must monitor their Corporate Capture deposit list queues for business customer deposits regularly throughout the day.

15.4	Enrolling and Managing Corporate Capture Business Customers
Business customers must sign an agreement with your financial institution to use this service before initiating a Corporate Capture deposit. See Section 3.7, Agreements for Corporate Capture Deposit Processing.
Financial institutions that have implemented Corporate Capture use the Corporate Capture Business Customer Enrollment and Maintenance Request, Form 8996, to enroll new business customers in Corporate Capture or to manage locations and/or accounts for an existing business customer. If you haven芒聙聶t implanted Corporate Capture, see Section 15.2, Enrolling in Corporate Capture, for more information.
Note:	Corporate Capture business customer set-up and maintenance requests are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Complete the Corporate Capture Business Customer Enrollment and Maintenance Request form as follows. See Figure 5 and Figure 6.
Note:	Enrollment requests can take up to ten business days to process.
General Information
芒聭
Enter your financial institution芒聙聶s name.
芒聭隆
Enter your customer芒聙聶s business name (e.g., Joe芒聙聶s Hardware). Maximum of 50 characters.
芒聭垄
Enter a short description of your customer芒聙聶s business location. For example, the city the business is in or an area of a city (e.g., downtown). Maximum of 50 characters.
芒聭拢
Select the appropriate request type.
Financial Institution 芒聙聯 Deposit Slip Settlement and Return Information (CAD/US)
Note:	Central 1 completes the Location and Unlisted Transit fields.
Location
芒聙聯 A unique identifier assigned to each of the business customers芒聙聶 location that displays in the Location drop-down menu in Corporate Capture. Business customers select the appropriate location when creating a deposit in Corporate Capture. The location format is as follows: 芒聙聹CC芒聙聺 + the business customer芒聙聶s 5-digit unlisted transit + 2-character region code + 芒聙聹Location 芒聙聯芒聙聺+ the city of the depositor芒聙聶s account (e.g., CC12345BC Location-Vancouver).
Unlisted Transit
芒聙聯 A unique transit and route number assigned to the business customer芒聙聶s location. User Management Security Officers use the unlisted transit when assigning the business customer芒聙聶s Corporate Capture User ID, password, and permissions in User Management. Unlisted transits aren芒聙聶t issued by or registered with Payments Canada and dishonoured items aren芒聙聶t returned to these numbers.
Fields 5-8 correspond to your financial institution芒聙聶s deposit slip settlement and return route/transits. Contact your financial institution芒聙聶s cheque deposit and clearing operations for assistance if required.
芒聭陇
Enter the 9-digit transit for the business customer芒聙聶s CAD Corporate Capture deposit settlement.
芒聭楼
Enter the 9-digit transit for the business customer芒聙聶s CAD Corporate Capture returns settlement.
芒聭娄
Enter the 9-digit transit for the business customer芒聙聶s USD Corporate Capture deposit settlement.
芒聭搂
Enter the 9-digit transit for the business customer芒聙聶s USD Corporate Capture returns settlement.
Business Customer Banking System Information
Fields 9-14 contain customer identifiers and deposit account details. If entering multiple accounts, ensure that you enter them in priority order. Accounts display in the same order in the Corporate Capture account drop-down menu as you list them on this form.
Note:	There are separate sections for
Canadian and US dollar accounts. The following field descriptions apply for both currencies.
芒聭篓
A legacy field. Only applies to real-time integration if your banking system expects the customer identifier in the ISO message PAN field. Ensure that you enter a non-card number. You can芒聙聶t enter a card number or alias that芒聙聶s subject to change. Leave this field blank if not applicable.
芒聭漏
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s customer identifier for your banking system.
芒聭陋
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s branch for your banking system.
芒聭芦
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s 9-digit transit for your banking system.
芒聭卢
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s deposit account number (the account to which deposits are made). If the account number is fewer than 30-digits, you don芒聙聶t need to zero-fill this field.
芒聭颅
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s deposit account transit.
Figure 5: Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Business Customer Enrollment and Maintenance, Form 8996, Page 1
Figure 6: Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Business Customer Enrollment and Maintenance, Form 8996, Page 2
Central 1 completes the Central1 UID/deposit accounts field for each account. This is used to set up the business customer芒聙聶s accounts in Corporate Capture. Corporate Capture UIDs (unique IDS) are a combination of a 2-digit currency code + the business customer芒聙聶s 9-digit unlisted transit + account priority order + 3-character currency code + an internal identifier (e.g., 03-080912345-001-CAD-DB for the Canadian dollar account in line 1, 03-080912345-002-CAD-DB for the Canadian dollar account in line 2). The customer芒聙聶s account number at your financial institution doesn芒聙聶t display in Corporate Capture. See Figure 7 for an example.
Figure 7: Example Corporate Capture Account Number
Create a request ticket in ServiceNow and attach the form to the request.

. See Figure 8.

15.5	Installing, Updating, or Testing a Corporate Capture Scanner Driver, and Corporate Capture Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting
Installing, Updating, and Testing a Corporate Capture Scanner Driver
Before creating a deposit in Corporate Capture, business customers must install a scanner driver onto a designated computer. After installing the scanner driver, a business customer芒聙聶s Corporate Capture User must test the scanner芒聙聶s functionality and ensure that the computer, the scanner, and Corporate Capture are interacting correctly.
Business customer procedures on installing, updating, and testing the scanner driver are available in Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Installing or Updating and Testing the Scanner Driver, Form 5933. You can share this user guide with your business customers.
Corporate Capture Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting
Business customer procedures for scanner maintenance and troubleshooting are available in Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting Guide, Form 5935. You can share this user guide with your business customers. The guide provides the following information:
Scanner cleaning and maintenance
Using the scanner diagnostics tool
Image quality issues
Reducing and resolving scanner jams
Minimizing image errors
Re-starting scanners
Replacing scanners
See Chapter 6, Branch Capture and Corporate Capture Scanner Procurement, for information on obtaining scanners for your financial institution and your business customers.
Related Information
Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Installing or Updating and Testing the Scanner Driver,
Form 5933
Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting Guide,
Form 5935
15.6	Corporate Capture User Management
Your financial institution芒聙聶s User Management Security Officer creates Corporate Capture Users for your own staff and for business customers. User Management Security Officers must have administrative rights to Corporate Capture to create these users. See the User Management Part for instructions. Use the descriptions in Section 15.3, Corporate Capture Workflows and User Types, to determine how best to assign Corporate Capture User types.
Important
:	Financial institutions that choose to review and transmit Corporate Capture deposits on behalf of their business customers must ensure that staff members who are processing Corporate Capture deposits aren芒聙聶t Branch Capture Users (e.g., Capture User, Reviewer, or Supervisor).
The only exception to this rule is the Branch Capture Administrator user type. The Branch Capture Administrator doesn芒聙聶t directly participate in the Branch Capture deposit process but does have access to reports and research for all your financial institution locations. Because business customers enrolled in Corporate Capture essentially become an extension of your financial institution, Administrator users can access business customer locations when generating reports.
15.7 	Corporate Capture Business Customer Training
Your financial institution is responsible for providing Corporate Capture training to your business customers. It芒聙聶s important that business customers understand their responsibilities, as business customers enrolled in Corporate Capture essentially become an extension of your financial institution.
This includes, but isn芒聙聶t limited to:
Knowing what deposit items can be processed in Corporate Capture. See Section 5.3, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Corporate Capture.
Knowing how to verify information on deposit items. See Section 5.4 Verifying Information on Cheques.
Ensuring Canadian and US deposit items are separated, and items are scanned using the appropriate deposit type.
Ensuring deposits are error-free and balanced before transmitting them to Central 1. Once a deposit is transmitted, it can芒聙聶t be deleted or recalled.
Ensuring deposits are transmitted to Central 1 before 7 pm local time to receive credit for the current business day, Monday through Friday (excluding holidays). Deposits transmitted outside these times will receive credit the next business day.
Ensuring business customers comply with your financial institution芒聙聶s policy on the retention and destruction of items processed via Corporate Capture. See Section 4.9, Policy on Retention and Destruction of Items Processed via Remote Deposit Capture.
Procedures for business customers are available in the Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Capture User Guide, Form 5932, Corporate Capture Supervisor User Guide, Form 5934, Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Capture and Supervisor User Guide, Form 5931, and Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Analyst User Guide, Form 5930. These user guides can be given to your participating business customers. These user guides are role芒聙聭specific. See Section 15.3, Corporate Capture Workflows and User Types, for a description of user types.
Corporate Capture training videos are also available on the Client Centre. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and other Resources.
Important
:	Share links to the unlisted training videos directly from YouTube. Don芒聙聶t share the Client Centre links to business customers, as they don芒聙聶t have access to the Client Centre
Related Information

15.8	Logging in to Corporate Capture
To access Corporate Capture, business customers and financial institution staff must have a Client Centre username and password. Your financial institution芒聙聶s User Management Security Officer creates the Corporate Capture user and forwards the username and password to the individual in a secure manner. See Section 15.6, Corporate Capture User Management.
Business Customer Corporate Capture Login
Business customers log in to Corporate Capture as follows:
Log in to Corporate Capture via the URL provided by Central 1.
Enter their username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 14 and Figure 15.
Figure 14: Client Centre Login
Figure 15: Corporate Capture Home Page
Financial Institution Staff Who Are Corporate Capture Users
Since Corporate Capture shares common attributes with Branch Capture, financial institution staff who are Corporate Capture Users can access Corporate Capture via the Branch Capture application on the Client Centre. See Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture, for procedures on accessing Branch Capture. Only financial institution staff can access Corporate Capture via Branch Capture on the Client Centre.
Important
: 	If your financial institution chooses to review and transmit Corporate Capture deposits on behalf of your business customers, ensure that staff who process Corporate Capture deposits aren芒聙聶t Branch Capture Users. The Corporate Capture application and Branch Capture application share common attributes but have different workflows.
15.9	Resolving User Login Issues
If the message 芒聙聹User ID of Remote Request was Invalid芒聙聺 displays when a Corporate Capture user first logs in to the application, verify the following:
The user芒聙聶s profile is granted access to Corporate Capture and the parameters are entered correctly in CPS Admin or User Management. See the CPS Admin Application Part or User Management Part for procedures.
Edits to the user芒聙聶s profile aren芒聙聶t pending the approval of a second User Management Security Officer. Go to the Dual Custody tab on CPS Admin or the Manage Requests option in User Management to verify. See the CPS Admin Application Part or User Management Part for procedures.
20 minutes have passed after the Corporate Capture User芒聙聶s profile is created or edited in CPS Admin or User Management. The profile changes need time to sync to the Client Centre.
15.10	Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting for Corporate Capture
The Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting Guide, Form 5935, provides the following information for Corporate Capture:
Scanner cleaning and maintenance

See Chapter 6, Branch Capture and Corporate Capture Scanner Procurement, for information on obtaining scanners for your financial institution and your business customers.
Related Information
Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting Guide,
Form 5935
15.11	Corporate Capture Deposit File
Central 1 provides a deposit file to financial institutions participating in Corporate Capture. If your financial institution uses real-time messaging, you can use the deposit file to reconcile transactions communicated to the host banking system. If your financial institution doesn芒聙聶t use real-time messaging, you can use the deposit file to credit Corporate Capture deposits automatically or manually to your business customers芒聙聶 accounts. This file contains the depositing account number (i.e., your business customer芒聙聶s account number), the transit number, and the amount of each deposit.
Your financial institution receives the deposit file specifications when you enroll in Corporate Capture. To view the deposit file specifications, see Corporate Capture XML Deposit File Specifications, Form 9349.
To obtain the deposit file, go to Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange and download the following files from the root directory:
RDCCmmdd.dat for Canadian dollar deposits
RDUCmmdd.dat for US dollar deposits
See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for procedures on accessing the File and Report Exchange. The file is in XML format and is generally available by 11 pm PT on the business day that Central 1 receives and processes the Corporate Capture deposits.
Related Information
Corporate Capture XML Deposit File Specifications,
Form 9349
END
